title: Querying Podman API inside an existing container (via Sockets, or System Service?)
body:
### Issue Description

I am attempting to query/use the Podman API inside a container, and am running into an issue where I can't access the API at all and the connection gets denied:

Some info about statuses and all that first:

```
$ systemctl --user status podman.socket podman.service
● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.socket; enabled; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: active (listening) since Wed 2024-08-21 19:36:20 BST; 44min ago
     Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
   Listen: /run/user/365767/podman/podman.sock (Stream)
   CGroup: /user.slice/user-365767.slice/user@365767.service/podman.socket

Aug 21 19:36:20 DEVBOX044088 systemd[1496]: Listening on Podman API Socket.

● podman.service - Podman API Service
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.service; enabled; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: inactive (dead) since Wed 2024-08-21 20:03:53 BST; 16min ago
     Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
  Process: 3041453 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman $LOGGING system service (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
 Main PID: 3041453 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)

Aug 21 20:03:48 DEVBOX044088 systemd[1496]: Starting Podman API Service...
Aug 21 20:03:48 DEVBOX044088 systemd[1496]: Started Podman API Service.
Aug 21 20:03:48 DEVBOX044088 podman[3041453]: time="2024-08-21T20:03:48+01:00" level=info msg="/usr/bin/podman filtering at log level info"
Aug 21 20:03:48 DEVBOX044088 podman[3041453]: time="2024-08-21T20:03:48+01:00" level=info msg="Setting parallel job count to 13"
Aug 21 20:03:48 DEVBOX044088 podman[3041453]: time="2024-08-21T20:03:48+01:00" level=info msg="Using systemd socket activation to determine API endpoint"
Aug 21 20:03:48 DEVBOX044088 podman[3041453]: time="2024-08-21T20:03:48+01:00" level=info msg="API service listening on \"/run/user/365767/podman/podman.sock\". URI: \"/run/user/365767/podman/podman.sock\""
Aug 21 20:03:48 DEVBOX044088 podman[3041453]: @ - - [21/Aug/2024:20:03:48 +0100] "GET /v4.4.0/libpod/info HTTP/1.1" 200 4423 "" "curl/7.61.1"
```

Testing on my box works fine:
```
$ curl -v --unix-socket /run/user/365767/podman/podman.sock http://d/v4.4.0/libpod/info
*   Trying /run/user/365767/podman/podman.sock...
* Connected to d (/run/user/365767/podman/podman.sock) port 80 (#0)
> GET /v4.4.0/libpod/info HTTP/1.1
> Host: d
> User-Agent: curl/7.61.1
> Accept: */*
>
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Api-Version: 1.41
< Content-Type: application/json
< Libpod-Api-Version: 4.4.1
< Server: Libpod/4.4.1 (linux)
< X-Reference-Id: 0xc0000c4e88
< Date: Wed, 21 Aug 2024 19:21:19 GMT
< Transfer-Encoding: chunked
<
{"host":{"arch":"amd64","buildahVersion":"1.29.3", ... etc
```

Version: (I have no control over being able to upgrade this to something new, that choice is beyond my powers.)
```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.4.1
API Version:  4.4.1
Go Version:   go1.19.13
Built:        Mon Apr 22 12:15:48 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

I then attempt to pull up a container as follows: `podman run --security-opt label=disable -d -p ${HOST_PORT}:${CONTAINER_PORT} -v /run/user/365767/podman/podman.sock:/run/podman/podman.sock -v log-volume:/tmp --env LINUX_ENV=${LINUX_ENV} --name ${CONTAINER_NAME} --restart=always ${REGISTRY_URL}/${IMAGE_NAME}:${TAG}`

According to the docs, which works fine and brings up container.. however when I do the following: `podman exec -it my-container-name /bin/bash` and login and try:
```
[containeruser@4f4cd2c06ff8 app]$ curl --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://d/v4.4.0/libpod/info -v
* Uses proxy env variable NO_PROXY == '10.0.0.0/8,127.0.0.1,.domain.com,.domain2.com,.domain3.com,.nom'
*   Trying /run/podman/podman.sock...
* Immediate connect fail for /run/podman/podman.sock: Permission denied
* Closing connection 0
curl: (7) Couldn't connect to server
```
The goal as in the title is to be able to run and get a response however I am unable to do so.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I'm not sure how to reproduce it, just that I'm probably doing something wrong? Unsure if the container's username needs to match with the host systems username at all either? 

### Describe the results you received

Error message saying I cannot connect/no valid response

### Describe the results you expected

Query response to my info request same as on host.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

RHEL 8.8

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
As an update I thought I could use the `podman system service` module to try and do this: 
`podman system service tcp:localhost:8080 --log-level=debug --time=0` and then a simple `curl http://localhost:8080/v4.4.1/libpod/info` to get the access. This works fine on the host, but not inside the container.

I also tried doing a `host.containers.internal` inside the container but made no difference. I thought TCP was supposed to allow me to do this?

> I also tried doing a host.containers.internal inside the container but made no difference. I thought TCP was supposed to allow me to do this?

You need to make the server listen on 0.0.0.0 or the specific host ip used by `host.containers.internal`, accessing the hosts localhost from the container is not allowed by default.

--- 

You permissions is because the socket is owned by your host uid which is mapped to root in the container, if you run as specific user in the ctr you will not have access. IF you do not want to run as root in the container then you need to use something like --userns keep-id, also see https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/debug-rootless-podman-mounted-volumes

> You need to make the server listen on 0.0.0.0 or the specific host ip used by `host.containers.internal`, accessing the hosts localhost from the container is not allowed by default.

Perfect! That was the missing piece. Thank you **so** much!
